chapter_summary: God rests on the seventh day, then forms Adam from dust, plants the Garden of Eden, creates Eve as Adam's companion, and establishes marriage.
main_lesson: God cares personally for people — He made us carefully, gave us a home, and made us for relationship.
memory_verse: It is not good that the man should be alone.
parent_guide: This chapter shows God's personal care: He breathes life into Adam, plants a garden just for him, and creates Eve because 'it is not good to be alone.' Great conversation starter: God made us for relationship.
application_for_children: God made you on purpose, gave you a home, and wants you to love people around you.

verses.kjv: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.
verses.little_bible: God finished making everything — the sky, the earth, and all the creatures.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God was done making everything.
verses.meaning: Creation was complete and perfect — every star, creature, and blade of grass was exactly as God intended.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for finishing Your amazing creation.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that God 'finished' His work?
verses.family_discussion: How can we notice the amazing things God made today?
verses.do_it_today: Go outside and look at something God made. Tell Him thank you.
verses.action_challenge: Draw your favourite thing God made.
verses.illustration_prompt: A peaceful completed creation scene — sky, land, water, animals — bathed in golden light, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.
verses.little_bible: On the seventh day, God stopped working and rested from everything He had made.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: On the seventh day, God rested.
verses.meaning: God set a pattern of rest — work and then rest — for all people to follow. Even the Creator of the universe paused to rest.
verses.memory_phrase: God rested on the seventh day.
verses.prayer: God, help me follow Your example and rest in You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God rested even though He never gets tired?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family rest together? What does good rest look like?
verses.do_it_today: Take a quiet rest today and thank God for all He has made.
verses.action_challenge: Plan one thing your family will do together to rest and enjoy God's world.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm sunrise over a peaceful landscape — all creatures resting quietly, a gentle glow in the sky, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.
verses.little_bible: God blessed the seventh day and made it special — a holy day of rest.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made the seventh day a special, holy day.
verses.meaning: God set this day apart as holy — different from all other days. Rest is not laziness; it is worship and trust in God.
verses.memory_phrase: God made the seventh day holy.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for giving us a day to rest and be with You.
verses.discussion_question: What makes a day 'holy' or special?
verses.family_discussion: How can we make our day of rest feel special and close to God?
verses.do_it_today: Do something today that helps you feel close to God — read, sing, or sit quietly.
verses.action_challenge: Make a 'rest day' plan with your family this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A family of animals resting peacefully under a big tree as soft light falls around them, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: These are the generations of the heavens and of the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made the earth and the heavens,
verses.little_bible: This is the story of how the sky and the earth were made by God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Here is the story of how God made the sky and the earth.
verses.meaning: This verse marks a new section of the story — zooming in on how God personally created and cared for the earth and the people in it.
verses.memory_phrase: God made the sky and the earth.
verses.prayer: Lord God, thank You that everything has a beginning in You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the Bible tells the creation story more than once?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing God made everything change how you see the world around you?
verses.do_it_today: Pick up a rock or a leaf and remember: God made this.
verses.action_challenge: Find three things outside and say 'God made this' for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A sweeping view from space of the earth and sky coming into focus — gentle and majestic, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And every plant of the field before it was in the earth, and every herb of the field before it grew: for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground.
verses.little_bible: Before any plants grew, there was no rain yet and no one to take care of the ground.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Before plants grew, there was no rain and no person yet.
verses.meaning: God prepared everything in order. People and rain were needed for plants to grow — God had a perfect plan for how everything would work together.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for planning everything so carefully.
verses.discussion_question: What do plants need to grow? Who provides all of that?
verses.family_discussion: How does God provide for plants? How does He provide for us?
verses.do_it_today: Water a plant and thank God for the rain He sends.
verses.action_challenge: Plant a seed together and watch God help it grow.
verses.illustration_prompt: Dry, bare ground with tiny seeds waiting — no rain, no plants yet — a sense of anticipation in the scene, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.
verses.little_bible: A mist rose up from the ground and watered all the land.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A gentle mist watered all the ground.
verses.meaning: Before rain, God used a mist to water the earth. He always finds a way to care for His creation.
verses.prayer: God, You always find a way to take care of us. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen mist in the morning? Where do you think it comes from?
verses.family_discussion: How does God water and care for the earth even when we don't notice?
verses.do_it_today: Look for dew or mist in the morning. Remember that God cares for the earth.
verses.action_challenge: Draw a picture of mist rising from the ground.
verses.illustration_prompt: Soft morning mist rising from dark rich soil, with the first green shoots just beginning to appear, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
verses.little_bible: God shaped a man from the dust of the ground and breathed life into him — and the man came alive!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made a man from dust and breathed life into him.
verses.meaning: God made people in the most personal way — hands on, breathing His own breath into us. We are not accidents; we are lovingly handcrafted by God.
verses.prayer: God, You made me with Your own hands. Thank You for the gift of life.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that God 'breathed life' into Adam?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing God personally formed us change how we see ourselves and others?
verses.do_it_today: Take a deep breath and remember — every breath is a gift from God.
verses.action_challenge: Count how many times you breathe in one minute. Each breath is a gift from God!
verses.illustration_prompt: God's hands gently shaping a figure from red-brown earth, a warm golden breath flowing from above into the figure, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
verses.little_bible: God planted a beautiful garden called Eden and put the man He had made right in it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made a garden called Eden and put the man there.
verses.meaning: God gave Adam a home — not a bare field, but a lush, beautiful garden planted by God Himself. He provides for us generously.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for giving me a home and people to be with.
verses.discussion_question: What would you put in your perfect garden?
verses.family_discussion: How has God given our family a home and a place to belong?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for your home — wherever you live.
verses.action_challenge: Draw the Garden of Eden as you imagine it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A lush, green garden bursting with colour — tall trees, bright flowers, a gentle river — with Adam standing in the middle, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.
verses.little_bible: God grew beautiful trees with delicious fruit in the garden — including the tree of life and a special tree He told them not to eat from.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made beautiful trees. One was the tree of life, and one was a tree not to eat from.
verses.meaning: God filled Eden with good things — beauty and food. But He also placed two special trees that would carry great meaning for Adam and Eve's choice.
verses.memory_phrase: God made trees beautiful and good.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for making beautiful things for us to enjoy.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God put a tree that was 'not to eat from' in the garden?
verses.family_discussion: What does having rules show us about God's love?
verses.do_it_today: Eat a piece of fruit today and thank God for making it grow.
verses.action_challenge: Look up what kinds of fruit trees grow in your country.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two magnificent trees standing in the centre of a lush garden — one glowing with golden light (tree of life), one with a mysterious aura, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.
verses.little_bible: A river flowed through Eden to water the garden, and it split into four rivers.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A river flowed through Eden and split into four rivers.
verses.meaning: God made sure the garden had everything it needed — even a river system to keep the whole land watered and alive.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for rivers, rain, and clean water.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen a river? What does a river do for the land?
verses.family_discussion: How does God provide clean water for people today?
verses.do_it_today: Drink a glass of water and thank God for it.
verses.action_challenge: Find out where your drinking water comes from.
verses.illustration_prompt: A clear sparkling river flowing through a lush garden, splitting into four streams heading in different directions, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: The name of the first is Pison: that is it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
verses.little_bible: The first river was called Pishon, and it flowed around a land that had gold.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The first river was called Pishon.
verses.meaning: God named and ordered creation — even the rivers had names and purposes. The detail shows God's attentiveness to His world.
verses.prayer: God, You know and name everything You have made. You know my name too.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think rivers have names?
verses.family_discussion: God knows every river and every person. How does that make you feel?
verses.do_it_today: Look on a map for a river near where you live.
verses.action_challenge: Draw a map of a made-up land with four rivers.
verses.illustration_prompt: A wide shimmering river winding through a golden landscape with glittering specks of gold in the riverbed, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the gold of that land is good: there is bdellium and the onyx stone.
verses.little_bible: That land had good gold, sweet-smelling resin, and precious onyx stones.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The land had gold and precious stones.
verses.meaning: God made a world full of precious things — beauty and wealth built into the very ground. Creation reflects God's generous nature.
verses.memory_phrase: God filled the earth with good things.
verses.prayer: God, Your world is full of beautiful and precious things. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: What precious or beautiful thing have you found or seen?
verses.family_discussion: How does the beauty in creation show us something about who God is?
verses.do_it_today: Find something beautiful in nature — a rock, a flower, a shell — and thank God for it.
verses.action_challenge: Collect five pretty rocks or natural treasures and display them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Glistening gold nuggets, creamy resin drops, and polished onyx stones nestled in the rich earth, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the name of the second river is Gihon: the same is it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia.
verses.little_bible: The second river was called Gihon, and it wound through the land of Cush.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The second river was called Gihon.
verses.meaning: God's rivers reached far-off lands — Eden was not isolated but connected to the whole world through these rivers.
verses.prayer: God, You care for people in every land and every country.
verses.discussion_question: Can you find Ethiopia (Cush) on a map?
verses.family_discussion: God loves people in every country. How can we pray for people far away?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for a child in a country you've never visited.
verses.action_challenge: Look up a country on a map and pray for the children there.
verses.illustration_prompt: A wide meandering river circling a dry, sunlit landscape with distant rolling hills, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the name of the third river is Hiddekel: that is it which goeth toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river is Euphrates.
verses.little_bible: The third river was the Hiddekel, flowing east toward Assyria. The fourth was the Euphrates.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The third river was the Hiddekel and the fourth was the Euphrates.
verses.meaning: These rivers were real places — the Bible is not a fairy tale. God created a real world for real people.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that the Bible tells us real stories about Your real world.
verses.discussion_question: Have you heard of the Euphrates river? Where is it?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing Bible places were real help us trust the Bible?
verses.do_it_today: Find the Euphrates River on a map or atlas.
verses.action_challenge: Draw the four rivers of Eden on a simple map.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two great rivers flowing powerfully through ancient landscapes — one east toward mountains, one broad and winding, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.
verses.little_bible: God placed the man in the garden to take care of it and look after it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God put the man in the garden to look after it.
verses.meaning: God gave Adam a purpose from the beginning — to tend and care for the garden. Work is a gift, not a punishment. God made us to be caretakers of His world.
verses.memory_phrase: God gave us work to do.
verses.prayer: God, help me take good care of the things You have given me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to take care of something?
verses.family_discussion: What has God given our family to take care of?
verses.do_it_today: Take care of something today — a plant, an animal, or your room.
verses.action_challenge: Find something in your home or garden that needs looking after. Do it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Adam tending the garden with gentle hands — watering plants, clearing a path — surrounded by animals watching peacefully, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat:
verses.little_bible: God told the man: 'You can eat from any tree in the garden freely!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God said Adam could eat from any tree in the garden.
verses.meaning: Before God gave a command of restriction, He gave generous permission. God is not a God of rules only — He is a God of great generosity.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for giving me so many good things to enjoy.
verses.discussion_question: How many trees do you think were in the garden? God said Adam could eat from ALL of them!
verses.family_discussion: How has God been generous with our family?
verses.do_it_today: List five good things God has given you. Thank Him for each one.
verses.action_challenge: Make a 'Good Things God Gave Me' list and put it on your wall.
verses.illustration_prompt: Adam reaching joyfully up to a tree full of bright colourful fruit — an expression of freedom and delight, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.
verses.little_bible: But God said: 'Do not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil — if you do, you will die.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God said do not eat from one special tree, or you would die.
verses.meaning: God gave one boundary among countless gifts. Trusting God means trusting that His boundaries are for our good, even when we don't fully understand them.
verses.memory_phrase: God asks us to trust and obey.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust that Your rules are always for my good.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God gave one rule?
verses.family_discussion: How does following rules show we trust someone?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a rule your parents have given you. Thank them for it.
verses.action_challenge: Talk about one family rule and why it protects you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A single distinctive tree standing apart, its fruit glowing with a mysterious light — a gentle sense of warning around it, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.
verses.little_bible: God said, 'It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a perfect helper for him.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God said it was not good for the man to be alone.
verses.meaning: For the first time in creation, God said something was 'not good' — and it was loneliness. God designed us for relationship. We are not meant to walk through life alone.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for giving me people to be with. Help me be a good friend.
verses.discussion_question: Who are the people God has given you?
verses.family_discussion: Why did God say it was not good to be alone?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone you love that you're glad God made them.
verses.action_challenge: Write a note to a friend or family member saying why you are glad God put them in your life.
verses.illustration_prompt: Adam standing alone in the beautiful garden, surrounded by pairs of animals — a gentle look of longing on his face, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.
verses.little_bible: God made all the animals and brought them to Adam to name. Whatever Adam called each creature, that became its name.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God brought all the animals to Adam, and Adam named them.
verses.meaning: God gave Adam the honour and responsibility of naming every creature — a sign of Adam's unique place in creation as God's representative on earth.
verses.memory_phrase: God trusted Adam with a big job.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for trusting people with important responsibilities.
verses.discussion_question: If you could name a new animal, what would you call it?
verses.family_discussion: How does giving someone an important job show that you trust them?
verses.do_it_today: Make up funny names for five animals you know.
verses.action_challenge: Create a 'name book' for five imaginary animals you invent.
verses.illustration_prompt: A parade of colourful animals — lions, elephants, birds, fish — approaching Adam who is thoughtfully giving each one a name, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.
verses.little_bible: Adam named every animal — but none of them was a perfect companion for him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Adam named every animal, but none was the right companion.
verses.meaning: Animals are wonderful, but they cannot fully meet the human need for companionship. God made people uniquely for each other.
verses.prayer: God, You know exactly what I need. Thank You for providing for me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think no animal was a perfect companion for Adam?
verses.family_discussion: What do you think makes a person different from an animal?
verses.do_it_today: Think of the people in your life — friends, family. Thank God for them.
verses.action_challenge: Draw Adam with all the animals and then draw the gap where Eve will be.
verses.illustration_prompt: Adam surrounded by all kinds of animals — every species present — but a subtle sense of something missing, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof;
verses.little_bible: God put Adam into a deep sleep, took one of his ribs, and closed the place up again.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God put Adam to sleep and took a rib from his side.
verses.meaning: God is like a wise and loving surgeon — He worked carefully and painlessly to give Adam the greatest gift. Eve would come not from Adam's head (above him) or his feet (below him), but from his side — beside him.
verses.prayer: God, You care for us even when we are asleep. Thank You for watching over us.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever needed a doctor to help you? How did they care for you?
verses.family_discussion: How does God care for us even when we are not aware of it?
verses.do_it_today: Before you go to sleep tonight, thank God for watching over you.
verses.action_challenge: Find out where your ribs are and how many you have.
verses.illustration_prompt: Adam peacefully sleeping in a soft beam of light, God's gentle presence nearby as He carefully forms something from Adam's side, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the rib, which the LORD God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.
verses.little_bible: God shaped the rib into a woman and brought her to the man.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made the woman from the rib and brought her to Adam.
verses.meaning: God Himself acted as the one who gives the bride — presenting Eve to Adam. Marriage is God's idea and God's gift.
verses.memory_phrase: God made us for each other.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for the people You have placed beside me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God brought the woman to Adam instead of just making her appear?
verses.family_discussion: How does God bring people together in families and friendships?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for one specific person He has placed beside you.
verses.action_challenge: Draw God presenting Eve to Adam in the garden.
verses.illustration_prompt: A radiant woman standing in a beam of light as God gently presents her to a wide-eyed, delighted Adam, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And Adam said, This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.
verses.little_bible: Adam said, 'She is like me — made from my own bone and flesh! She will be called Woman because she came from Man.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Adam said: she is like me — she will be called Woman.
verses.meaning: Adam's first words about Eve are a poem of recognition and joy. He saw that she was uniquely like him — and uniquely for him.
verses.memory_phrase: God makes us to belong together.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for making people who understand and belong with each other.
verses.discussion_question: How did Adam feel when he saw Eve? How do you know?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to belong to a family?
verses.do_it_today: Say 'I'm glad you're in my family' to someone you love today.
verses.action_challenge: Write a short poem or sentence about someone you love.
verses.illustration_prompt: Adam looking at Eve with wonder and joy, speaking words that form in the air between them like light, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
verses.little_bible: That is why a man and woman leave their families, come together as husband and wife, and become one family.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: That is why a man and woman leave their parents and start a new family together.
verses.meaning: God instituted marriage here — a lifelong, exclusive bond between a man and a woman. This is the foundation of the family.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for families. Bless my family and every family.
verses.discussion_question: What makes a family special?
verses.family_discussion: How does God care for our family?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for your family — whoever they are.
verses.action_challenge: Draw a picture of your family and write one thing you love about each person.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man and woman standing together as one, hands joined, surrounded by the beauty of the garden, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.
verses.little_bible: Adam and Eve had no need to be ashamed — they were perfectly made and perfectly loved by God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Adam and Eve were not ashamed. They were perfectly loved by God.
verses.meaning: Before sin entered, there was no shame — no hiding, no fear, no guilt. This is the peace God wants to restore to us through Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You love me completely and I don't need to hide from You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to be with someone you can be completely honest and safe with?
verses.family_discussion: How does Jesus help us come back to God without shame?
verses.do_it_today: Tell God one thing you have been afraid to say. Remember — He already knows and still loves you.
verses.action_challenge: Thank God for one thing about yourself that He made.
verses.illustration_prompt: Adam and Eve in the golden garden, at peace — a scene of innocence, joy, and complete belonging, storybook watercolor style.
